title: Iconic Brand Palettes & Classic Color Schemes
description: Famous brand/product color palettes with verified hex codes, plus classic design-system palettes and developer color themes.

entries.name: Spotify
entries.summary: Spotify's brand palette pairs a single vivid green with near-black and white for a high-energy, dark-first look.
entries.significance: One of the most recognizable single-color brands in tech; the green reads as 'alive/growth' against the app's dark background and is used for the logo, play button, and primary actions.
key_facts: Brand black is #191414, not pure black.
White #FFFFFF completes the core three-color system.

entries.name: Slack
entries.summary: Slack combines a deep aubergine base with a four-color hashtag mark in cyan, green, yellow, and magenta.
entries.significance: The 2019 rebrand standardized aubergine as the anchor and gave the octothorpe logo four fixed accent hues used across marketing and UI.
key_facts: Logo accents: blue #36C5F0, green #2EB67D, yellow #ECB22E, red #E01E5A.

entries.name: Stripe
entries.summary: Stripe's identity centers on a purple-leaning indigo that deliberately avoids the navy most fintech brands use.
entries.significance: The distinctive indigo plus generous whitespace and gradient accents became a widely imitated 'developer product' aesthetic.

entries.name: Airbnb
entries.summary: Airbnb's signature coral-red 'Rausch' anchors a warm, friendly palette.
entries.significance: The 2014 Belo rebrand named the color 'Rausch' after 19 Rausch Street, the address of the founders' first hosted stay.
key_facts: Named after 19 Rausch Street, San Francisco.

entries.name: Coca-Cola
entries.summary: Coca-Cola's identity is built on a single bright red paired with white.
entries.significance: One of the oldest and most globally recognized single-color brand systems; 'Coca-Cola Red' is effectively synonymous with the product.
key_facts: Coca-Cola red is commonly documented as #F40009 (sources vary slightly, e.g. #FE001A).

entries.name: Tiffany & Co. (Tiffany Blue)
entries.summary: Tiffany Blue is a trademarked robin's-egg blue-green used on the brand's boxes and bags.
entries.significance: A rare example of a legally protected brand color; standardized with Pantone as a private custom color named '1837 Blue' after the company's founding year.
key_facts: Pantone custom color '1837 Blue', named for the 1837 founding year.
Registered as a color trademark in 1998; not sold in standard Pantone swatch books.

entries.name: Meta / Facebook
entries.summary: Facebook's long-standing blue was refreshed to a brighter Meta blue in the 2021 corporate rebrand.
entries.significance: 'Facebook Blue' defined a generation of social-web UI; the color was reportedly chosen partly because founder Mark Zuckerberg is red-green colorblind.

entries.name: Twitter / X
entries.summary: The platform shifted from a light-blue bird identity to a monochrome black-and-white 'X' in 2023.
entries.significance: Illustrates a dramatic palette pivot: the iconic '1DA1F2' Twitter blue was largely replaced by black/white after the X rebrand.
key_facts: X brand is primarily black #000000 on white #FFFFFF.

entries.name: IBM (Carbon Blue)
entries.summary: IBM's Carbon Design System centers on 'Blue 60' as the primary interactive color across a full blue ramp.
entries.significance: Carbon's blue and neutral gray ramps are among the most influential enterprise UI color systems.
key_facts: Carbon defines a 10-step blue scale from Blue 10 to Blue 100.

entries.name: Google (Four Colors)
entries.summary: Google's identity uses four primary hues — blue, red, yellow, green — in the logo and product icons.
entries.significance: The four-color mark signals approachability and has anchored Google's identity since the late 1990s; the current flat values were set with the 2015 logo refresh.

entries.name: Netflix
entries.summary: Netflix uses a bold red on near-black, a high-contrast cinematic pairing.
entries.significance: 'Netflix Red' is a defining streaming-era brand color; the dark UI foregrounds cover art.
key_facts: Netflix Red is #E50914 per brand logo guidance.

entries.name: Notion
entries.summary: Notion's brand is near-monochrome: black on white/off-white with muted, low-saturation content accents.
entries.significance: Popularized a restrained, editorial black-and-white product aesthetic; content colors are intentionally desaturated to avoid competing with user data.
key_facts: Primary is black #000000 / near-black #191919 on white.
In-app accent swatches are muted pastels rather than saturated brand hues.

entries.name: Linear
entries.summary: Linear pairs a violet-indigo brand accent with a refined dark-gray UI.
entries.significance: Linear's dark, high-craft aesthetic and indigo accent became a reference point for modern developer-tool design.
key_facts: Brand accent is an indigo/violet around #5E6AD2.
UI is built on a neutral dark-gray ramp.

entries.name: Discord (Blurple)
entries.summary: Discord's identity is anchored by 'Blurple', a blue-purple hybrid, on a dark neutral UI.
entries.significance: The 2021 rebrand shifted Blurple from #7289DA to a brighter, more accessible #5865F2.

entries.name: Cash App
entries.summary: Cash App uses a single bright, high-chroma green as its dominant brand color.
entries.significance: The vivid green signals money and immediacy and is used near-monochromatically across the app.

sources.title: The Cash App Logo, Colors and Meaning

entries.name: Mailchimp (Cavendish Yellow)
entries.summary: Mailchimp's brand centers on 'Cavendish' yellow paired with near-black.
entries.significance: A rare high-visibility yellow-led identity; the yellow-plus-black system is distinctive in the SaaS space.

entries.name: Instagram (Gradient)
entries.summary: Instagram's 2016 rebrand replaced its skeuomorphic camera with a warm purple-to-orange gradient mark.
entries.significance: Popularized multi-stop brand gradients; the sunset gradient became widely imitated across app branding.
key_facts: Gradient runs roughly from purple/blue through magenta to orange/yellow.
Common stops include #405DE6, #833AB4, #C13584, #E1306C, #FD1D1D, #F77737, #FCAF45 (exact stops vary by asset).

entries.name: Firefox
entries.summary: Firefox's identity uses a warm orange-to-magenta gradient wrapping a stylized fox.
entries.significance: The 2019 Firefox brand system introduced a gradient-based logo family spanning the browser and related products.
key_facts: Logo gradient extends into red/magenta and yellow.

entries.name: Twitch
entries.summary: Twitch uses a saturated purple as its dominant brand color on a dark UI.
entries.significance: The purple 'Glitch' mark is instantly associated with live game streaming.

entries.name: YouTube
entries.summary: YouTube's identity is built on a bright red play button on white or dark backgrounds.
entries.significance: The red play triangle is one of the most recognized UI marks in the world.
key_facts: YouTube red is #FF0000 (the 2017 refresh moved the red into the play button).

entries.name: Material Design Color Palette
entries.summary: Material Design's original palette defines a set of hues (Red, Pink, Blue, Green, etc.) each with tonal steps from 50 to 900 plus accent values.
entries.significance: The 50-900 numeric tonal ramp became a de-facto convention for organizing UI color scales across the industry.
key_facts: Representative 500-level swatches: Red #F44336, Pink #E91E63, Purple #9C27B0, Indigo #3F51B5, Blue #2196F3, Teal #009688, Green #4CAF50, Amber #FFC107.
Each hue defines steps 50, 100–900 plus A100–A700 accents.
Material 3 later replaced fixed swatches with dynamic tonal palettes generated from a seed color.

entries.name: Tailwind CSS Default Palette
entries.summary: Tailwind ships a default palette of named hues (slate, red, blue, green, etc.), each as an 11-step 50–950 ramp.
entries.significance: Tailwind's carefully-tuned ramps became the default color vocabulary for a large share of modern web UIs; v4 redefined the swatches in the OKLCH color space for wider-gamut vibrancy.
key_facts: Representative 500-level swatches: Red #EF4444, Amber #F59E0B, Green #22C55E, Blue #3B82F6, Indigo #6366F1, Slate #64748B.
Tailwind v4 (2025) defines the default palette in OKLCH.

entries.name: Radix Colors
entries.summary: Radix Colors is an open-source system of 12-step scales engineered for specific UI roles (backgrounds, components, borders, solids, text).
entries.significance: Its role-based 12-step model (rather than arbitrary shades) and APCA-based contrast targets made it a reference for accessible UI color systems.
key_facts: Each scale has 12 steps: backgrounds (1–2), interactive components (3–5), borders (6–8), solid colors (9–10), accessible text (11–12).
Ships 22 color scales, 6 grays, plus transparent variants (336 colors total).
Contrast targets use the APCA algorithm.

entries.name: iOS / Apple System Colors
entries.summary: Apple's Human Interface Guidelines define semantic system colors (systemBlue, systemGreen, etc.) that adapt between light and dark appearances.
entries.significance: System colors give apps a consistent, accessible, appearance-aware palette; the light-mode default values are widely referenced.
key_facts: Light-mode defaults: systemBlue #007AFF, systemGreen #34C759, systemRed #FF3B30, systemOrange #FF9500, systemYellow #FFCC00, systemPink #FF2D55, systemPurple #AF52DE, systemIndigo #5856D6.
Values shift automatically in Dark Mode and for increased-contrast settings.

entries.name: Nord
entries.summary: Nord is a cool, arctic-inspired 16-color palette grouped into Polar Night, Snow Storm, Frost, and Aurora.
entries.significance: A very popular editor/terminal theme known for its muted, low-contrast blue-gray aesthetic.

entries.name: Dracula
entries.summary: Dracula is a dark theme with a purple-tinted background and a set of vivid pastel accents.
entries.significance: One of the most widely ported dark developer themes, with a formal spec covering dozens of apps.
key_facts: Accents: cyan #8BE9FD, green #50FA7B, orange #FFB86C, pink #FF79C6, purple #BD93F9, red #FF5555, yellow #F1FA8C.

entries.name: Solarized
entries.summary: Solarized is a sixteen-color palette (light and dark modes) built on carefully measured CIELAB relationships.
entries.significance: Influential for its methodical, perceptually-tuned design: the base tones share fixed lightness relationships so light and dark modes use the same accent hues.
key_facts: Base tones: base03 #002B36, base02 #073642, base01 #586E75, base00 #657B83, base0 #839496, base1 #93A1A1, base2 #EEE8D5, base3 #FDF6E3.
Accents: yellow #B58900, orange #CB4B16, red #DC322F, magenta #D33682, violet #6C71C4, blue #268BD2, cyan #2AA198, green #859900.

entries.name: Catppuccin
entries.summary: Catppuccin is a soothing pastel theme with four flavors — Latte (light), Frappe, Macchiato, and Mocha (darkest).
entries.significance: A modern community theme known for balanced pastel accents; widely ported across editors, terminals, and apps.
key_facts: Mocha accents include rosewater #F5E0DC, mauve #CBA6F7, red #F38BA8, green #A6E3A1, blue #89B4FA, yellow #F9E2AF.
Four flavors: Latte, Frappe, Macchiato, Mocha.

entries.name: Gruvbox
entries.summary: Gruvbox is a retro-groove theme with warm, earthy tones and both dark and light variants.
entries.significance: Popular for its warm, low-eye-strain palette that avoids the cool blues of most dark themes.
key_facts: Accents: red #CC241D, green #98971A, yellow #D79921, blue #458588, purple #B16286, aqua #689D6A, orange #D65D0E.

entries.name: Pantone Color of the Year (Overview)
entries.summary: Since 2000, Pantone has named an annual Color of the Year intended to reflect the cultural moment and influence design across industries.
entries.significance: A widely-watched trend signal that ripples through fashion, product, and graphic design each year.

sources.title: Every Pantone Color of the Year

entries.name: Pantone Classic Blue (2020)
entries.summary: Classic Blue was Pantone's 2020 Color of the Year, a calm, dependable dusk-sky blue.
entries.significance: Chosen to convey reassurance and stability entering a new decade.

sources.title: Every Pantone Color of the Year

entries.name: Pantone Very Peri (2022)
entries.summary: Very Peri was a periwinkle blue with a violet-red undertone, the first COTY Pantone created new rather than selecting from existing colors.
entries.significance: Notable as a purpose-designed color reflecting a digitally-influenced moment.

sources.title: Every Pantone Color of the Year

entries.name: Pantone Viva Magenta (2023)
entries.summary: Viva Magenta was a bold crimson-red 2023 Color of the Year signaling energy and boldness.
entries.significance: Represented a shift toward vivid, assertive color after several muted years.

sources.title: Every Pantone Color of the Year

entries.name: Pantone Peach Fuzz (2024)
entries.summary: Peach Fuzz was a soft, warm pastel peach chosen as 2024 Color of the Year.
entries.significance: Emphasized gentleness, comfort, and connection.

sources.title: Every Pantone Color of the Year

entries.name: Pantone Mocha Mousse (2025)
entries.summary: Mocha Mousse was a warm, rich brown chosen as 2025 Color of the Year.
entries.significance: Reflected a move toward comforting, grounded earth tones.

sources.title: Pantone 2025 Color of the Year is Mocha Mousse
